root_text.an6.44:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena migasālāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an6.44:1.2: Atha kho migasālā upāsikā yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho migasālā upāsikā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.44:2.1: “Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyaṁ?
root_text.an6.44:2.2: Pitā me, bhante, purāṇo brahmacārī ahosi ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.
root_text.an6.44:2.3: So kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato sakadāgāmī satto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapannoti.
root_text.an6.44:2.4: Petteyyopi me, bhante, isidatto abrahmacārī ahosi sadārasantuṭṭho.
root_text.an6.44:2.5: Sopi kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato sakadāgāmipatto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapannoti.
root_text.an6.44:2.6: Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyan”ti?
root_text.an6.44:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando migasālāya upāsikāya nivesane piṇḍapātaṁ gahetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.an6.44:3.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.44:4.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena migasālāya upāsikāya nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁ; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdiṁ.
root_text.an6.44:4.2: Atha kho, bhante, migasālā upāsikā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho, bhante, migasālā upāsikā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.44:4.3: ‘kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyaṁ.
root_text.an6.44:4.4: Pitā me, bhante, purāṇo brahmacārī ahosi ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.
root_text.an6.44:4.5: So kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato sakadāgāmipatto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapannoti.
root_text.an6.44:4.6: Petteyyopi me, bhante, isidatto abrahmacārī ahosi sadārasantuṭṭho.
root_text.an6.44:4.7: Sopi kālaṅkato bhagavatā byākato sakadāgāmipatto tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapannoti.
root_text.an6.44:4.8: Kathaṁ kathaṁ nāmāyaṁ, bhante ānanda, bhagavatā dhammo desito aññeyyo, yatra hi nāma brahmacārī ca abrahmacārī ca ubho samasamagatikā bhavissanti abhisamparāyan’ti?
root_text.an6.44:4.9: Evaṁ vutte, ahaṁ, bhante, migasālaṁ upāsikaṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.an6.44:5.1: “Kā cānanda, migasālā upāsikā bālā abyattā ammakā ammakapaññā, ke ca purisapuggalaparopariyañāṇe?
root_text.an6.44:5.2: Chayime, ānanda, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an6.44:6.2: Idhānanda, ekacco puggalo sorato hoti sukhasaṁvāso, abhinandanti sabrahmacārī ekattavāsena.
root_text.an6.44:6.3: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
root_text.an6.44:6.4: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti no visesāya, hānagāmīyeva hoti no visesagāmī.
root_text.an6.44:7.1: Idha panānanda, ekacco puggalo sorato hoti sukhasaṁvāso, abhinandanti sabrahmacārī ekattavāsena.
root_text.an6.44:7.2: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
root_text.an6.44:7.3: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti no hānāya, visesagāmīyeva hoti no hānagāmī.
root_text.an6.44:8.2: ‘imassapi teva dhammā aparassapi teva dhammā, kasmā tesaṁ eko hīno eko paṇīto’ti.
root_text.an6.44:8.3: Tañhi tesaṁ, ānanda, hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
root_text.an6.44:9.1: Tatrānanda, yvāyaṁ puggalo sorato hoti sukhasaṁvāso, abhinandanti sabrahmacārī ekattavāsena, tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
root_text.an6.44:9.2: Ayaṁ, ānanda, puggalo amunā purimena puggalena abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an6.44:9.9: Ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ, yo vā panassa mādiso.
root_text.an6.44:10.1: Idha panānanda, ekaccassa puggalassa kodhamāno adhigato hoti, samayena samayañcassa lobhadhammā uppajjanti.
root_text.an6.44:10.2: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi akataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi appaṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
root_text.an6.44:10.3: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti no visesāya, hānagāmīyeva hoti no visesagāmī.
root_text.an6.44:11.1: Idha panānanda, ekaccassa puggalassa kodhamāno adhigato hoti, samayena samayañcassa lobhadhammā uppajjanti.
root_text.an6.44:13.1: Idha panānanda, ekaccassa puggalassa kodhamāno adhigato hoti, samayena samayañcassa vacīsaṅkhārā uppajjanti.
root_text.an6.44:13.2: Tassa savanenapi akataṁ hoti …pe… sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ na labhati.
root_text.an6.44:13.3: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā hānāya pareti no visesāya, hānagāmīyeva hoti no visesagāmī.
root_text.an6.44:14.1: Idha panānanda, ekaccassa puggalassa kodhamāno adhigato hoti, samayena samayañcassa vacīsaṅkhārā uppajjanti.
root_text.an6.44:14.2: Tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
root_text.an6.44:14.3: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā visesāya pareti no hānāya, visesagāmīyeva hoti no hānagāmī.
root_text.an6.44:15.2: ‘imassapi teva dhammā, aparassapi teva dhammā. Kasmā tesaṁ eko hīno, eko paṇīto’ti?
root_text.an6.44:15.3: Tañhi tesaṁ, ānanda, hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
root_text.an6.44:16.1: Tatrānanda, yassa puggalassa kodhamāno adhigato hoti, samayena samayañcassa vacīsaṅkhārā uppajjanti, tassa savanenapi kataṁ hoti, bāhusaccenapi kataṁ hoti, diṭṭhiyāpi paṭividdhaṁ hoti, sāmāyikampi vimuttiṁ labhati.
root_text.an6.44:16.2: Ayaṁ, ānanda, puggalo amunā purimena puggalena abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an6.44:16.9: Ahaṁ vā, ānanda, puggalesu pamāṇaṁ gaṇheyyaṁ, yo vā panassa mādiso.
root_text.an6.44:17.1: Kā cānanda, migasālā upāsikā bālā abyattā ammakā ammakapaññā, ke ca purisapuggalaparopariyañāṇe.
root_text.an6.44:17.2: Ime kho, ānanda, cha puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an6.44:18.1: Yathārūpena, ānanda, sīlena purāṇo samannāgato ahosi, tathārūpena sīlena isidatto samannāgato abhavissa. Nayidha purāṇo isidattassa gatimpi aññassa.
root_text.an6.44:18.2: Yathārūpāya ca, ānanda, paññāya isidatto samannāgato ahosi, tathārūpāya paññāya purāṇo samannāgato abhavissa. Nayidha isidatto purāṇassa gatimpi aññassa.
root_text.an6.44:18.3: Iti kho, ānanda, ime puggalā ubho ekaṅgahīnā”ti.

translation_text.an6.44:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the laywoman Migasālā, where he sat on the seat spread out.
translation_text.an6.44:1.2: Then the laywoman Migasālā went up to Ānanda, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an6.44:2.1: “Honorable Ānanda, how on earth are we supposed to understand the teaching taught by the Buddha, when the chaste and the unchaste are both reborn in exactly the same place in the next life?
translation_text.an6.44:2.2: My father Purāṇa was chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.
translation_text.an6.44:2.3: When he passed away the Buddha declared that, since he was a once-returner, he was reborn in the host of joyful gods.
translation_text.an6.44:2.4: But my uncle Isidatta was not chaste; he lived content with his wife.
translation_text.an6.44:2.5: When he passed away the Buddha declared that, since he was also a once-returner, he was reborn in the host of joyful gods.
translation_text.an6.44:2.6: How on earth are we supposed to understand the teaching taught by the Buddha, when the chaste and the unchaste are both reborn in exactly the same place in the next life?”
translation_text.an6.44:2.7: “You’re right, sister, but that’s how the Buddha declared it.”
translation_text.an6.44:3.1: Then Ānanda, after receiving almsfood at Migasālā’s home, rose from his seat and left.
translation_text.an6.44:3.2: Then after the meal, on his return from almsround, Ānanda went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.an6.44:5.1: “Ānanda, who is this laywoman Migasālā, a foolish incompetent aunty, with an aunty’s wit? And who is it that knows how to assess individual persons?
translation_text.an6.44:5.2: These six individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an6.44:6.2: Take a certain individual who is gentle and pleasant to be with. And spiritual companions enjoy living together with them.
translation_text.an6.44:6.3: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.
translation_text.an6.44:6.4: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher. They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.
translation_text.an6.44:7.1: Take another individual who is gentle and pleasant to be with. And spiritual companions enjoy living together with them.
translation_text.an6.44:7.2: And they’ve listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found temporary freedom.
translation_text.an6.44:7.3: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower. They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.
translation_text.an6.44:8.2: ‘This one has just the same qualities as the other, so why is one worse and one better?’
translation_text.an6.44:8.3: This will be for their lasting harm and suffering.
translation_text.an6.44:9.1: In this case, the individual who is gentle … and has listened, learned, comprehended theoretically, and found temporary freedom
translation_text.an6.44:9.2: is better and finer than the other individual.
translation_text.an6.44:9.4: Because the stream of the teaching carries them along.
translation_text.an6.44:9.5: But who knows the difference between them except a Realized One?
translation_text.an6.44:9.6: So, Ānanda, don’t be judgmental about individuals.
translation_text.an6.44:9.8: Those who pass judgment on individuals harm themselves.
translation_text.an6.44:9.9: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on individuals.
translation_text.an6.44:10.1: Take another individual who is angry and conceited, and from time to time has greedy thoughts.
translation_text.an6.44:10.2: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.
translation_text.an6.44:10.3: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher. They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.
translation_text.an6.44:11.1: Take another individual who is angry and conceited, and from time to time has greedy thoughts. …
translation_text.an6.44:11.2: Because the stream of the teaching carries them along. …
translation_text.an6.44:11.3: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re headed for a higher place, not a lower. They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.
translation_text.an6.44:12.2: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on people.
translation_text.an6.44:13.1: Take another individual who is angry and conceited, and from time to time has the impulse to speak inappropriately.
translation_text.an6.44:13.2: And they’ve not listened or learned or penetrated theoretically or found even temporary freedom.
translation_text.an6.44:13.3: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a lower place, not a higher. They’re going to a lower place, not a higher.
translation_text.an6.44:14.1: Take another individual who is angry and conceited, and from time to time has the impulse to speak inappropriately.
translation_text.an6.44:14.2: But they’ve listened and learned and penetrated theoretically and found temporary freedom.
translation_text.an6.44:14.3: When their body breaks up, after death, they depart for a higher place, not a lower. They’re going to a higher place, not a lower.
translation_text.an6.44:15.2: ‘This one has just the same qualities as the other, so why is one worse and one better?’
translation_text.an6.44:15.3: This will be for their lasting harm and suffering.
translation_text.an6.44:16.1: In this case, the individual who is angry and conceited, but has listened, learned, comprehended theoretically, and found temporary freedom
translation_text.an6.44:16.2: is better and finer than the other individual.
translation_text.an6.44:16.4: Because the stream of the teaching carries them along.
translation_text.an6.44:16.5: But who knows the difference between them except a Realized One?
translation_text.an6.44:16.6: So, Ānanda, don’t be judgmental about individuals.
translation_text.an6.44:16.8: Those who pass judgment on individuals harm themselves.
translation_text.an6.44:16.9: I, or someone like me, may pass judgment on individuals.
translation_text.an6.44:17.1: Who is this laywoman Migasālā, a foolish incompetent aunty, with an aunty’s wit? And who is it that knows how to assess individual persons?
translation_text.an6.44:17.2: These six individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an6.44:18.1: If Isidatta had achieved Purāṇa’s level of ethical conduct, Purāṇa could not have even known Isidatta’s destination.
translation_text.an6.44:18.2: And if Purāṇa had achieved Isidatta’s level of wisdom, Isidatta could not have even known Purāṇa’s destination.
translation_text.an6.44:18.3: So both individuals were lacking in one respect.”

variant_text.an6.44:5.1: ammakā ammakapaññā → ambakā ambakapaññā (bj); ambakā ambakasaññā (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
